item.title: first off, welcome to my new kitchen!!! I’m beyond grateful to...
item.description:
View on Instagram


first off, welcome to my new kitchen!!! I’m beyond grateful to be able to live in San Diego and so close to the beach. Huge ‘WTF is my life’ kind of moment. maybe I’ve had a couple breakdowns and avoided the camera and mirror due to the new stupid bangs but FCK IT. It’ll grow out. As you can tell there’s a lot I wanna share but this has been my favorite way to reset lately. Moving across the country was.. a lot. More cooking, horngry, and snowboarding videos soon 🫶🏼Sandwich details: -your fav bread -thinly sliced turkey breast -CRISPY bacon -avocado (mashed w salt, pepper) -sharp cheddar -red onions -micro greens -random homemade ranch I threw together lol

item.title: I’ve never laughed harder in my life 😭🤣 my friends are s...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I’ve never laughed harder in my life 😭🤣 my friends are such troopers !! Filmed on my @gopro MAX using the narwhal mount! #beginnerskier #skiing #snowboarder #winter #gopro

item.title: short ribs 😏 your girl is back and horngry !! this video was ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


short ribs 😏 your girl is back and horngry !! this video was the last one I filmed in my Richmond place, a couple days before moving. :( videos of my new kitchen and what I’ve been up to coming soon !!! Love u all 🫶🏼🫶🏼🫶🏼

item.title: the reversible OnlyPans, extra horngry and ~crazy~ apron is now ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


the reversible OnlyPans, extra horngry and ~crazy~ apron is now available in my bio!! PS there are pockets on both sides and a towel loop on the front! I went to @hedleyandbennett with the idea of a reversible apron because I wanted it to be simple, but also very hectic with a ton of graphics (welcome to my brain). So they combined my two dreams into this masterpiece!! It took a while to think of how we could make this work without it being too thick, or looking awkward on one side. But after many drafts and calls we finally landed on this piece of perfection. Thank you @elinpk for making my art vision come to life. go check her out and buy some art! and most importantly thank YOU for being here. Here’s to some messy cooking in the future, I love you all !!!

item.title: #AD Virtual Family Dinner! Simplicity is behind @bertolli_us cul...
item.description:
View on Instagram


#AD Virtual Family Dinner! Simplicity is behind @bertolli_us culinary tradition of making dinner spectacular. Enjoy Italian flavors made from simple ingredients for over 150 years. :) Recipe below! #SimpleistheBertolliWay #FamilyDinner Ingredients: -Bertolli Alfredo -Chicken breast (olive oil, salt, pepper, italian seasoning) -Fettuccine -Optional: parsley, fresh parmesan, red chili flakes Recipe: -pour Bertolli Alfredo sauce in a pot on low heat. bring to a small simmer -Butterfly chicken to make thinner breasts. Season generously with salt, pepper, and italian seasoning -Heat a pan on medium-high heat. add olive oil. Cook each side of the chicken for about 3 minutes. *cook longer if you’re using a thicker piece of meat. remove chicken and let rest -Bring a large pot of water to a boil. add salt and the pasta. -Stir together sauce and cooked pasta. top with grilled chicken and parsley! ENJOY :)) **Highly recommend making this with friends or family members!

item.title: GIMME ALL THE ROLLS !!! Recipe below!! (it’s simply homecooked...
item.description:
View on Instagram


GIMME ALL THE ROLLS !!! Recipe below!! (it’s simply homecooked recipe) Ingredients: -4cups all purpose flour -1/4cup sugar + 1tbsp -1tsp salt -3/4-1tsp cinnamon -1/2cup unsalted softened butter -3/4cup warm whole milk -1 1/2 tsp active dry yeast -3 eggs -egg wash: 1 egg and honey (1-2tsp) Recipe: -mix together warm milk, 1tbsp sugar, and yeast. Stir and let sit until foamy (10ish min) -to a stand mixer (with hook attachment) add flour, 1/4c sugar, salt, cinnamon, eggs, and yeast mixture. Mix on low, increasing to high until a ball is formed -add softened butter (in chunks) and slowly increase to high. Mix for 10min until the dough is no longer sticking to the sides (or do the windowpane test) -add to a greased bowl and set aside until doubled in size (60-90min) -deflate and cut into 24 equal (ish) pieces. It doesn’t need to be perfect I just mentally didn’t want to count that much so I weighed it out (total weight divided by 24!) -roll into balls and add to a buttered 9x13inch dish. Let proof for 1 hour -preheat oven to 350F. Brush on egg wash and bake for 25 min until golden brown on top. -optional to top with melted butter & salt :)

item.title: steak sandwich 😈 recipe below! Ingredients: -large baguette -...
item.description:
View on Instagram


steak sandwich 😈 recipe below! Ingredients: -large baguette -skirt steak -steak marinade: olive oil, lemon juice, worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, 1-2 garlic cloves, salt and pepper -garlic aioli: mayo, lemon juice, grated garlic clove, salt, pepper -caramelized onions: 2tbsp butter, 2 white onions, red wine, salt -arugula: tossed in lemon juice and olive oil -provolone cheese Recipe: -mix marinade ingredients! I usually do 1/3c olive oil, good amount of soy sauce and couple dashes of Worcestershire, 1/2 lemon juiced, 1-2 grated garlic clove, salt and pepper. Taste and adjust to your liking! I promise it’ll be delish. -mix steak in marinade, cover and set aside (put in the fridge if you won’t cook it within 2 hours) -slice onions. Add to a pan on medium heat. Add butter, onions and salt. Cover and turn to med-low. Stir occasionally. After 45 min remove lid and deglaze with red wine. Let cook until fully softened and browned in color -mix together aioli ingredients. Taste and adjust! I like mine super garlicky but you do you babe -cook steak in a cast iron on med-high. Each side for 3-4 min. Let rest for 5 minutes. Slice into thin pieces -to one side of the baguette, add a thin layer of aioli, top with sliced steak and provolone. Broil in the oven until the cheese is melted -add a generous amount of caramelized onions on top of the provolone. to the other side add aioli and arugula! Enjoy !!!! ❤️❤️❤️

item.title: My recent go-to simple, flavorful dip for gatherings! All my fri...
item.description:
View on Instagram


My recent go-to simple, flavorful dip for gatherings! All my friends LOVED this. Recipe below :) @ithacahummus #ad Ingredients: -1 container of Ithaca’s black bean hummus -2 ears of corn -1/4 white onion, diced -1/4 jalapeño, diced-1/2 avocado, diced (or just do the whole avo, it’s delish) -1 grated garlic clove -1/2 lime, juiced -olive oil -pinch of salt, pepper and cumin -toppings: cilantro, cotija cheese-serve with: tortilla chips, pita bread, veggies, or just eat with a spoon Recipe: -heat cast iron on medium-high. Add olive oil to the corn and cook each side for about 3 min -remove and cut corn off the cob -combine diced onions, avocado, jalapeño, and garlic to the corn. Add olive oil, lime juice, salt, pepper and cumin. Mix to combine -add hummus to a bowl, top with corn mixture, cilantro and cotija cheese. Drizzle olive oil on top and serve with tortilla chips, pita or veggies!!!
